original_reasoning: Arabic 'anatil = hyenas that tear prey to pieces; also 'hard, severe'. Greek aneilonto = 3pl aor middle of anaireo 'they took up, killed, destroyed'. The 'taking-by-tearing/killing' link is direct in the destructive-action field. Strong link.

الأنف=أعرض عن الحق (turned away from truth) / يتتبع أنفه (follows his nose = arrogant). φλήναφος=idle talk, nonsense. Arrogant disregard for truth and speaking nonsense share the domain of rejecting truth/substance, though from different angles.
ختربه 'cut it up' gives an action, and ὄχθοιβος is a stripe on a tunic, plausibly the cut result.

البينيث = سمك بحري = a sea fish. πολυβενθής = very deep (of sea). A sea fish lives in the βένθος (depth). The Arabic names the creature; the Greek describes its habitat. The deep-sea semantic field is shared directly. Fish belong to depth; depth hosts fish.

طسّ = ذهب (went away, disappeared); also غطّسه في الماء = submerged him in water. τελευτή = end, death, completion. Disappearing/going away is semantically close to ending/death. Both express cessation of presence. The drift from 'vanishing' to 'death/end' is natural and well-attested.
Arabic also refers to what is used for pounding, so a split/asunder result is a plausible action-to-result development.
السخب is noisy clamor, and λέσχη includes talk or gossip, so both converge on loud social speech.
barid=posthorse/postal system vs veredus=posthorse; same semantic meaning, Arabic loaned from Greek/Latin
